water users and the men who col
onized the various government ir-
rigation projects. Garfield won
out repeatedly.


This year the situation is re-
versed. The secretary of the in
terror is on the defensive as usual,
but this time it is the water users
and the homeseekers who are the
aggressors, and the interests" are
behind Ballinger.


The charge against Secretary
Ballinger is, in substance, that he
has overturned the spirit of and is
attempting to block the Roosevelt
policies as pertaining to conserva-
ton and reclamation.


Ballinger will be asked to ex-
plain, first, why he is not satisfied
with the administration of Freder-
lex H. Newell as director of the
reclamation service. Director New
ell is the man who really conceived
the whole scheme of the reclama-
ton of the arid lands of the west.
Way back before Senator New
lands of Nevada and others sue
ceeded in getting the reclamation
act through congress, F. H. Newell,
then employed in the U. s. geolog-
ical survey, dreamed about rec
lamation. Men laughed at him
then, but he is now fast realizing
his dreams, as water is pouring
over the once desert lands of the
west.


The reclamation act was passed
In the same week as the Panama
canal act. and up to January /,
1909, the reclamation service had
moved more dirt than was moved
at Panama. Never since the work
began has man been employed
in the reclamation service for any
reason other than that he was the
best man available for the place.
Politicians who have had friends
to reward have been sent else.
where.

penditure of hundreds of thousands
